title: Address roborev findings for example plugin workflow
description: Fix RoboRev findings on the example plugin by sanitizing reflected input, adding direct binary tests for empty and non-empty stdin, and reducing duplicated example workflow steps across docs.

title: Fix Gemini plugin prompt and stdin mapping
description: Update gemini-plugin to preserve the Plugboard stdin contract while invoking Gemini in non-interactive mode with a fixed --prompt instruction, then verify the exact shell invocation against the installed Gemini CLI.
notes:
Implemented the adapter change so Plugboard message bodies remain on stdin while Gemini receives a fixed --prompt instruction and added tests for that contract. Shell validation in this Codex environment remains unresolved: plain 'gemini -p' examples return for the user, but timeout-bounded probes here still hang for the plugin-style JSON and approval-mode invocations.
Scope refined: simplify the Gemini adapter and docs around direct --prompt usage. Plugboard still delivers the message to the plugin on stdin, but the plugin will pass that body to Gemini as --prompt and stop forwarding stdin to the Gemini subprocess.
Simplified the adapter again: it now reads the worker-delivered message body from stdin, passes that body directly as Gemini's --prompt value, and does not forward stdin to the Gemini subprocess. Updated Gemini-specific docs to use the simplest user-facing example, 'gemini --prompt ...'. Cargo tests pass, but timeout-bounded direct Gemini probes from this Codex shell still do not reproduce the user's successful interactive runs.

title: Implement minimal Plugboard prototype
description:
Read the repository README.md and implement the smallest working prototype consistent with it.

Constraints:

- Plugboard is a local textual message exchange.
- Do not introduce agent abstractions or workflow engines.
- Do not introduce typed RPC protocols.
- Keep the system minimal and inspectable.
- Messages should be textual with simple metadata (for example topic, timestamp, id).

The first prototype should support:

- publishing a message
- listing or polling messages by topic
- claiming a message
- acknowledging completion
- publishing follow-up messages

Prefer simple local storage such as sqlite or files.

Keep the code small and readable.

title: Bootstrap Rust crate, dependencies, and project skeleton
description:
Create or update the project as a Rust binary crate with the expected top-level structure and minimal compilable scaffolding.

Required outcomes:
- Cargo.toml exists and is sane
- src/ tree exists
- module files exist with minimal scaffolding
- schema.sql exists, even if preliminary
- examples/ and tests/ directories exist
- the project compiles with placeholder logic

Dependency guidance:
Prefer a small set of boring dependencies only. Reasonable categories include:
- CLI parsing
- SQLite access
- time handling
- id generation
- error handling

Avoid:
- heavy async runtimes
- framework-like crates
- unnecessary serialization stacks unless clearly needed

Do not implement real business logic yet beyond what is needed for compilable scaffolding.

title: Add core unit tests for domain and exchange behaviour
description:
Add the main unit tests needed to keep the implementation honest.

Required outcomes:
- unit tests cover core logic around messages, claims, publish/read, claim transitions, and follow-up behaviour
- tests are located close to the logic where practical
- the test suite runs cleanly

Constraints:
- prefer focused tests over giant integration blobs
- do not rely only on manual verification

title: Add one small end-to-end integration test or example flow
description:
Add one compact demonstration that proves the core idea.

Required outcomes:
- either an integration test or an example program/script exists
- it shows a message being published
- a runner claims it
- a command executes
- a follow-up message is emitted
- the resulting conversation can be inspected

Constraints:
- keep the demo small
- do not build a showcase framework
- use an ordinary command if possible to keep the example understandable

title: Polish error handling, documentation comments, and final wiring
description:
Do the final cleanup needed for a credible v1 prototype.

Required outcomes:
- errors are reasonably readable
- module boundaries remain clear
- obvious rough edges in CLI or exchange wiring are cleaned up
- code comments or doc comments exist where they clarify intent
- the project builds and tests cleanly

Constraints:
- do not add new major features here
- keep polish proportional
- prefer clarity over cleverness

title: Define domain types for messages and claims
description:
Implement the small core domain types needed for v1.

Required outcomes:
- domain module exists
- Message type exists
- Claim type exists
- ClaimStatus enum exists
- constructors or helper functions exist where useful
- types reflect the architecture docs without adding extra concepts

Constraints:
- keep these types concrete and boring
- do not create Agent, Workflow, Job, Subscription, ExecutionPlan, or similar
- metadata may exist as an optional shallow field, but should not dominate the model
- message bodies are textual and primary

Tests:
- add unit tests for basic type construction where sensible

title: Create initial SQLite schema and database initialization
description:
Implement schema.sql and the database initialization path.

Required outcomes:
- schema.sql contains the initial messages and claims tables
- useful indexes exist, especially for topic and active claims
- exchange initialization can apply the schema
- local database setup is testable

Constraints:
- only messages and claims are durable tables in v1
- keep schema aligned with ARCHITECTURE.md
- do not add conversations table unless absolutely necessary
- do not add subscriptions, jobs, workflows, retries, schedules, or anything similar

Tests:
- add a test that initializes the schema successfully

title: Implement exchange publish and read operations
description:
Implement the exchange API for writing and reading messages.

Required outcomes:
- publish message operation exists
- read by topic exists
- read by conversation exists
- basic inspect/list support over messages exists
- conversation id propagation behaviour is implemented consistently with the architecture docs

Constraints:
- publishing appends a new message only
- publishing does not mutate prior messages
- if a message has no conversation_id, assign according to the architecture guidance
- follow-up messages should be able to inherit conversation context

Tests:
- publish/read round trip
- conversation id propagation
- reading by topic
- reading by conversation

title: Implement atomic claim logic by topic
description:
Implement transactional claiming of one eligible message for a topic.

Required outcomes:
- claim operation exists on the exchange API
- claiming is atomic and transactional
- a message with an active claim is not claimed twice
- the selection mechanism is simple and deterministic enough for v1

Constraints:
- do not implement list-then-claim as separate uncontrolled steps
- do not add priorities, fairness tuning, or scheduling
- an eligible message for v1 is simply one matching the topic with no active claim

Tests:
- first claim succeeds
- second concurrent-or-sequential attempt does not reclaim the same active message
- claiming non-existent eligible work behaves correctly

title: Implement claim completion, failure, and timeout transitions
description:
Implement the operational transitions for claims.

Required outcomes:
- complete claim operation exists
- fail claim operation exists
- timeout handling path exists at least at the exchange state transition level
- claim statuses transition cleanly
- completed_at is recorded where appropriate

Constraints:
- claim state is separate from message content
- do not mutate original messages
- keep state transitions explicit and small

Tests:
- complete transition
- fail transition
- timeout transition
- invalid transition handling if needed

title: Implement follow-up message creation for outcomes
description:
Implement the logic that appends follow-up messages after processing outcomes.

Required outcomes:
- success follow-up message creation exists
- failure follow-up message creation exists
- timeout follow-up message creation exists
- parent_id and conversation_id are propagated correctly

Recommended topic pattern:
- original topic: code.generate
- success: code.generated
- failure: code.generate.failed
- timeout: code.generate.timed_out

Constraints:
- append follow-up messages instead of mutating prior messages
- keep topic derivation simple
- do not build a workflow system around this

Tests:
- parent reference is correct
- conversation inheritance is correct
- topics are assigned correctly

title: Implement CLI commands for publish, read, and inspect
description:
Implement the first user-facing CLI commands as thin wrappers.

Required outcomes:
- publish command works
- read command works for topic and conversation
- inspect command works at a simple human-readable level
- main CLI wiring is coherent

Constraints:
- keep output simple and useful
- do not overdesign formatting
- CLI should call into application/exchange logic rather than embedding behaviour

Tests:
- add at least minimal integration coverage or command-level tests where practical

title: Implement the command runner loop
description:
Implement the minimal runner that turns a passive command into an asynchronous participant.

Required outcomes:
- run command accepts topic, success-topic, failure-topic, timeout, and child command
- runner polls for work
- runner claims one message
- runner passes message body to child stdin
- runner captures stdout, stderr, and exit status
- runner records claim outcome
- runner publishes follow-up output messages

Constraints:
- keep it single-message-at-a-time
- keep it synchronous in v1
- runner depends on exchange API, not SQL
- do not parse body text for routing decisions
- do not add concurrency pools, scheduling, retries, or backoff systems beyond a tiny sleep loop

Tests:
- success command path
- failing command path
- timeout path if practical

title: Map Gemini plugin message body to prompt
description: Update gemini-plugin so the claimed Plugboard message body is passed to Gemini as the --prompt value instead of stdin, add tests for the invocation contract, and document the rationale in source comments.
notes: Changed gemini-plugin so the Plugboard message body read from stdin becomes Gemini's --prompt value, with Gemini launched without forwarded stdin. Added integration and worker-host tests to verify the prompt mapping and the absence of stdin forwarding.

title: Align Gemini plugin docs and source comments
description: Update the Gemini plugin source comments and repository docs so they describe the confirmed stdin plus --prompt Gemini invocation model accurately.
notes: Updated gemini-plugin source comments plus README and Gemini how-to docs so they describe the confirmed stdin plus --prompt invocation model and the current shell-validation caveat accurately.

title: Worker Host and Plugin Architecture
description: Introduce a worker runtime and plugin system to enable asynchronous agent/tool execution on top of Plugboard topics, while keeping Plugboard core agent-agnostic.

title: Refactor run into worker host
description: Clarify worker responsibilities, isolate the polling and claim loop, and separate execution logic from CLI parsing so the runtime is a worker host rather than a generic subprocess helper.

title: Add practical quickstart and worker plugin docs
description: Add a short end-to-end quickstart and a concise how-to for writing worker plugins, then link both from the README without bloating the top-level project overview.

title: Remove markdown separators from README and architecture docs
description: Remove standalone horizontal-rule markdown separators from README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md to keep the documentation style cleaner and more consistent.

title: Define plugin interface
description: Introduce a minimal Rust plugin interface for worker execution. Input should include message data and context; output should be a structured success or failure result.

title: Implement command plugin
description: Preserve current stdin/stdout subprocess behavior as a command plugin. Ensure stdin is written then closed, stdout and stderr are captured, and exit codes are mapped into structured results.

title: Timeout and lifecycle handling
description: Enforce per-message timeout handling in the worker host and publish timeout follow-up messages on <topic>.timed_out.

title: Message to execution mapping
description: Define and document how message bodies and metadata map into execution inputs, including the decision to use stdin instead of arguments in the initial plugin contract.

title: Example plugin
description: Add a simple deterministic or API-backed example plugin that proves an end-to-end Plugboard topic to worker to plugin to follow-up flow.
notes: Added a deterministic example plugin binary at src/bin/example-review-plugin.rs, documented the local workflow in docs/quickstart.md and docs/howto/write-a-worker-plugin.md, and added an end-to-end integration test that runs the worker host against the example plugin binary.

title: Documentation alignment
description: Align README, architecture, and design documentation with the worker host and plugin architecture. Remove implications of identity-based delivery and add an end-to-end example.

title: CLI UX improvements
description: Clarify worker invocation in the CLI and help text, including whether to keep run or introduce a clearer worker-oriented command surface.

title: Handle BrokenPipe in command plugin stdin writes
description: The command plugin should not crash the worker host when a child command closes stdin early or ignores input. Treat BrokenPipe as a valid non-interactive command behavior and keep the plugin execution flow alive.

title: Add real Gemini worker plugin
description: Implement a real Gemini adapter plugin for the worker host, document its prerequisites and request/reply workflow, keep the deterministic example plugin clearly marked as a demo, and add realistic tests around the adapter logic.
notes:
Implemented a real gemini-plugin binary that invokes the installed Gemini CLI once per message in non-interactive JSON mode, added parser and fake-CLI integration tests, kept example-review-plugin clearly marked as a demo, rewrote the Codex-to-Gemini guide around the real plugin and explicit prerequisites, and attempted a live Gemini run which timed out without producing a response in this environment.
Added src/bin/gemini-plugin.rs as a real Gemini CLI adapter, added fake-CLI unit and end-to-end coverage, split docs so example-review-plugin stays clearly demo-only, and documented real Gemini prerequisites. Direct non-interactive Gemini CLI probes still timed out without output in this environment, so live end-to-end verification remains dependent on local Gemini auth/network health.

title: Address RoboRev findings for Gemini plugin
description: Add the remaining Gemini adapter fallback-path tests called out by RoboRev and reduce duplicated prerequisite text across the Gemini docs.
notes: Addressed RoboRev jobs 192, 193, and 195 by adding Gemini adapter fallback-path tests and deduplicating the Gemini prerequisite docs. Follow-up review job 197 passed with no findings.

title: Improve CLI help and document agent-style topic workflows
description: Make Plugboard understandable from the CLI by improving help text, adding a concrete Codex-to-Gemini style request/reply how-to, aligning docs, and verifying the documented topic-based workflow locally.
notes: Improved CLI help text for publish/read/run and top-level help, added a Codex-to-Gemini request/reply how-to using topic conventions and the local example plugin, aligned README/quickstart/architecture/design docs, and will verify the documented workflow with the built binaries.

title: Clarify stateless worker execution model in help and docs
description: Make it explicit in CLI help and practical docs that worker backends are invoked once per message, must read stdin, write stdout, and exit, and that Plugboard does not manage persistent sessions.
notes: Clarified that worker backends are stateless per-message executors in run help and practical docs: one fresh process per message, stdin/stdout/exit contract, no shared memory between runs, and no persistent sessions managed by Plugboard.
